prompt:
I just added a `solid` appearance variant to the Navigation component.
Did you document this variant in the playground? You almost never do.
Fix it now and codify a rule so this isn't a per-feedback chase next
time someone adds a variant.
response:
I have everything I need. The investigation is complete. Let me now deliver the full structured response. --- ## Diagnosis The playground page at `playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx` **already has both `ComponentPreview` blocks and the `PropsTable` entry for `solid`** — so the immediate documentation gap is *already closed* in the file. However: 1. **The `.solid` CSS class is absent from `Navigation.module.scss`.** The component conditionally applies `styles.solid` when `appearance === "solid"`, but that class is never defined in the SCSS file. The variant is wired but visually inert — the playground renders the correct label but shows *no visible difference* from `frosted` because no styles back it up. 2. **The systemic gap is real.** The variant was added to the TSX and the playground in the same session, but the SCSS was never touched. The feedback (EAP-082, guardrail-engineering-24) is about that exact failure mode: a variant exists in the API but is invisible or incomplete in practice. 3. **No enforcement hook exists at the task boundary.** There is no checklist step that asks "does the SCSS define every class the component conditionally applies?" before closing the work, which is why this keeps recurring. --- ## Root Cause **EAP-082 — Adding a Variant/Prop to a DS Component Without Updating the Playground Page** (and its mirror: without completing the *implementation* the variant declares). The specific failure is a subset of EAP-082: the variant was declared in the prop type (`NavigationAppearance = "frosted" | "solid"`), conditionally applied as a class in TSX (`styles.solid`), and documented in the playground — but the CSS class that makes the variant *do something* was never written. The guardrail (Engineering guardrail #24) says to update the playground page; the implicit corollary it doesn't yet spell out is: **the SCSS must define every class that the component's variant logic references.** --- ## Fix ### 1. Define the `solid` appearance in `Navigation.module.scss` The `frosted` (default) style uses `--portfolio-surface-terra-subtle` and the blurred `::after` spill-shadow. The `solid` variant should use an opaque surface, kill the blur tail, and remove the spill-shadow. Based on the SCSS's existing token vocabulary: ```scss // src/components/ui/Navigation/Navigation.module.scss // ─── After the existing .nav block ──────────────────────────────────────────── .solid { // Fully opaque surface — no transparency, no blur bleed. // background-color is already set by .nav; override box-shadow and kill // the blurred spill-shadow pseudo-element. box-shadow: none; // The ::after spill-shadow is only meaningful for frosted (translucent) // contexts where content slides under the nav. On a solid surface it // creates an unintended double-line artifact. &::after { display: none; } } ``` **Why only shadow changes and no background change?** `Navigation.module.scss` already sets `background-color: var(--portfolio-surface-terra-subtle)`. If "solid" means "opaque terra-05" per the playground description, and `terra-subtle` already is opaque (not a translucent rgba), then the only *visible* delta the `frosted` variant has over a plain block is the `::after` blur spill-shadow below the bar. Killing that and the box-shadow on `.solid` produces a clean, bounded surface with no blur artifacts. If you want to differentiate further (e.g. a slightly darker surface token or a 1px bottom border), add it here: ```scss .solid { box-shadow: none; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--portfolio-border-subtle); // optional hard edge &::after { display: none; } } ``` --- ### 2. The playground page is already correct — verify it renders the delta `playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx` already contains: ```tsx
``` And the `PropsTable` already declares `"frosted" | "solid"`. No changes needed there. After writing the SCSS, flush and restart per the playground rule: ```bash lsof -ti :4001 | xargs kill -9 rm -rf playground/.next npm run playground & # wait for ready, then: curl -s http://localhost:4001/components/navigation | grep 'Solid' ``` --- ### 3. Codify the systemic rule — update EAP-082 in `docs/engineering-anti-patterns.md` Add a **Sub-pattern** note immediately after the existing EAP-082 body: ```markdown ## EAP-082: Adding a Variant/Prop to a DS Component Without Updating the Playground Page **Sub-pattern EAP-082b — Declared class, missing definition:** A variant is considered complete only when ALL THREE of the following are true: 1. The prop/type is declared in the TSX (`NavigationAppearance`, etc.) 2. The conditional class application in TSX references a class that is **defined in the component's `.module.scss`** 3. The playground page has a `ComponentPreview` block rendering the variant Failing any one of the three means the variant is incomplete. The SCSS class is the most commonly forgotten leg — it compiles silently (TypeScript does not catch a missing CSS Module class; the value is just `undefined` at runtime, so no class is applied and no visual change occurs). **Checklist trigger:** Before closing any task that adds a variant or prop to a DS component, run: ``` grep -n "styles\." src/components/ui//.tsx ``` Then confirm every `styles.` reference has a matching `.` rule in `.module.scss`. If any are missing, define them before marking done. ``` --- ### 4. Promote guardrail-engineering-24 in `AGENTS.md` The current guardrail text (Engineering #24) reads approximately: > ALWAYS update the playground page when adding a new variant, prop, or visual state to any DS component. Extend it with an explicit SCSS leg: ```markdown 24. **ALWAYS** complete all three legs when adding a variant or prop to a DS component: (a) Declare the type/prop in the TSX. (b) Define every class the component conditionally applies in the component's `.module.scss` — TypeScript does NOT catch a missing CSS Module class; the value silently becomes `undefined`, the class is never applied, and the variant appears identical to the default. (c) Add a `ComponentPreview` block and `PropsTable` row to the playground page. All three must be done before the task is considered closed. See EAP-082. ``` --- ## Principle **EAP-082 / Guardrail-Engineering-24.** A variant is a three-legged contract: API declaration (TypeScript type + prop), implementation (SCSS class definition), and documentation (playground `ComponentPreview`). TypeScript enforces the first leg at compile time. The SCSS leg is completely silent — a missing class resolves to `undefined` at runtime, no class string is applied, and the variant renders identically to the default. The playground leg is visible only to someone who opens the page. Because only the first leg has automated enforcement, the other two are permanently at risk of being skipped. The SCSS grep-check above makes the second leg mechanically verifiable in one command; the playground Intent Gate already handles the third. Run all three before closing any variant-addition task — not as a review step, but as the definition of "done."
